data.title: COSCO SHIPPING Orders 12 New Vessels in RMB16.8 Billion Fleet Expansion
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from COSCO SHIPPING Holdings Co ( (HK:1919) ) is now available.

COSCO SHIPPING Holdings has commissioned the construction of 12 new vessels through its wholly owned subsidiary COSCO Asset Management, signing shipbuilding contracts with Jiangnan Shipyard and China Shipbuilding Trading worth a total of RMB16.79 billion. The ships, to be delivered between 2028 and 2029, will be financed by up to 60% external debt or bank loans with the balance from internal resources, and the contracts include detailed progress-based payment schedules, liquidated damages for delivery delays and technical underperformance, and refund protections in case of termination, underscoring the group’s continued fleet expansion and disciplined capital management under Hong Kong’s disclosable transaction rules.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1919) stock is a Sell with a HK$10.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on COSCO SHIPPING Holdings Co stock, see the HK:1919 Stock Forecast page.

More about COSCO SHIPPING Holdings Co

COSCO SHIPPING Holdings Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated, Hong Kong-listed shipping and logistics company engaged primarily in container shipping and related asset management through subsidiaries such as COSCO Asset Management, with a focus on global maritime transport services.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company ( (HK:0468) ) has provided an update.

Greatview Aseptic Packaging Company has announced that executive director Lee Hsien Dar Victor will resign from the board with effect from 13 January 2026 due to an internal realignment of responsibilities, with the company stressing there is no disagreement with the board or disclosure matters arising from his departure. The company serves beverage and liquid food producers and positions itself as a competitor in the global aseptic packaging market, with operations managed from its base in Beijing and incorporated in the Cayman Islands.

data.title: ETHK Labs Overhauls Board, Committees and Company Secretary After Senior Resignations
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

IVD Medical Holding Limited ( (HK:1931) ) has shared an announcement.

ETHK Labs Inc. has announced substantial changes to its leadership and governance structure effective 13 January 2026, with the resignation of executive directors Mr. Law Kim Fai and Ms. Tai Yang, and independent non-executive director Mr. Wong Sze Lok, alongside Mr. Law stepping down as chief financial officer, company secretary and authorised representative. In response, the company has reshuffled key board committees, appointing Mr. Zhang Jianlei as chairman of both the audit and remuneration committees, adding Mr. Chan Siu Kei Ken to the nomination committee and naming him company secretary, and appointing Mr. Zhong Renqian and Mr. Xu Da to the remuneration committee. The changes leave the nomination committee temporarily without gender diversity as required under Hong Kong’s corporate governance code, and the board has indicated it will review the committee’s composition, underscoring ongoing governance obligations and the importance of maintaining regulatory compliance amid senior management turnover.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1931) stock is a Hold with a HK$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on IVD Medical Holding Limited stock, see the HK:1931 Stock Forecast page.

More about IVD Medical Holding Limited

ETHK Labs Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, is part of a group engaged in business activities that require a formal board, audit, remuneration and nomination committee structure under Hong Kong’s Listing Rules and Corporate Governance Code, indicating a regulated, exchange-listed corporate profile focused on compliance and governance standards.

data.title: JD Logistics Moves to Delist Subsidiary Deppon from Shanghai, Offers RMB3.8 Billion Cash Option to Minority Shareholders
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

JD Logistics, Inc. ( (HK:2618) ) has provided an update.

JD Logistics plans to delist its consolidated subsidiary Deppon from the Shanghai Stock Exchange, with its wholly owned subsidiary Suqian Jingdong Zhuofeng, an indirect controlling shareholder of Deppon, proposing a voluntary withdrawal of Deppon’s shares subject to approval at a general meeting and by the Shanghai Stock Exchange. As part of the deal, another wholly owned JD Logistics unit will extend a cash option at RMB19.0 per share to acquire up to 19.76% of Deppon’s remaining shares from minority investors, implying a transaction value of about RMB3.8 billion if fully exercised; the move, which would increase JD Logistics’ ownership from about 80% and potentially pave the way for full control and privatisation of Deppon, is classified as a discloseable transaction in Hong Kong and may affect minority shareholder liquidity, pending regulatory and shareholder approvals.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2618) stock is a Buy with a HK$14.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on JD Logistics, Inc. stock, see the HK:2618 Stock Forecast page.

More about JD Logistics, Inc.

JD Logistics, Inc. is a China-based logistics and supply chain solutions provider listed in Hong Kong, offering integrated warehousing, transportation and delivery services. Through acquisitions such as that of Deppon Group, the company has expanded its presence in less-than-truckload, freight and integrated logistics services across mainland China, strengthening its role in the broader e-commerce and industrial logistics ecosystem.

data.title: Aidigong Shareholders Back Partial Board Shake-up, Leaving Listing Rule Breach
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Aidigong Maternal & Child Health ( (HK:0286) ) has provided an update.

Aidigong Maternal & Child Health reported the poll results of a special general meeting held on 13 January 2026, where shareholders voted on a sweeping reshuffle of the board. Resolutions to remove directors Huang Wenhua, Li Runping, Meng Lijia and Wang Bin, as well as to appoint Zhu Yufei as executive director and Yu Lin, Wang Jun and Jiang Yanbo as independent non-executive directors, were all rejected by a majority of votes, while the removal of directors Lin Jiang and Chu Pui Ki Dickson was approved with over 80% support. The mixed outcome leaves the company with only partial changes to its board and committees and triggers a period of non-compliance with Hong Kong listing rules on board composition, underscoring ongoing governance tensions and potential uncertainty for investors and other stakeholders.

More about Aidigong Maternal & Child Health

Aidigong Maternal & Child Health Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company (stock code: 286) incorporated in Bermuda, operating in the maternal and child health services industry. The group focuses on providing postpartum care and related health services for mothers and infants, targeting the growing market for specialised family and maternity wellness in Greater China.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$251.6M

CiDi Inc. Class H ( (HK:3881) ) has issued an announcement.

CiDi Inc. has amended its Articles of Association to reflect changes arising from the issuance and listing of its H shares on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on 19 December 2025. The revisions formalize the company’s updated share capital structure, including specifying 5,407,980 overseas listed foreign shares, setting registered capital at RMB 43,789,310 and total investment at RMB 76,762,660, and updating the total number of shares issued following its corporate restructuring. These adjustments, authorized by shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting in September 2024, align the company’s constitutional documents with regulatory requirements and its post-listing capital base, providing greater transparency and clarity for investors and regulators while leaving other provisions of the Articles unchanged.
